explanation: This command uses a reversed string 'tnirP' which, when reversed, becomes 'Print'. It simulates obfuscation via reversed commands as detected by the Sigma rule.
The reversed string 'ekovn' is included in the Sigma rule's detection list. This command reverses it to 'nvoke', which is part of 'Invoke-Command', simulating obfuscated PowerShell usage.
The reversed string 'llehsrewop' becomes 'PowerShell' when reversed. This command simulates the use of reversed commands to execute PowerShell, matching the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
The reversed string 'rahc' becomes 'char', which is part of 'Get-Content' or 'Get-Item' commands. This command simulates obfuscation via reversed commands as detected by the Sigma rule.
